subject: HSC Mathematics Extension 1
description: Index of marking guidelines by year. Each entry references the full marking guideline data embedded in the question bank. This file provides a quick lookup for the marking-guideline-feedback skill.

partialCreditPolicy.description: OpenTutor partial credit assessment policy, aligned with NESA principles

rules.condition: Correct first step but incorrect subsequent steps
Correct answer without required working shown
Follow-through error: error from part (a) carried correctly into part (b)
'Show that' question: answer is given, so full working is mandatory
rules.marksAwarded: 0 if working absent, even if conclusion is stated

rules.condition: Proof question: working both sides simultaneously
rules.marksAwarded: 0 — must work from one side only

ext1SpecificPolicies.description: Additional marking policies specific to Extension 1 question types

policies.policy: Full stepwise working required. Conclusion must follow logically from working. No marks for stating the result alone.
General solution must include arbitrary constant C. Particular solution requires explicit application of initial conditions.
Answers must clearly distinguish between scalars and vectors. Vector answers must include direction (e.g., component form or magnitude + direction).
Cartesian equation must include any restrictions on domain/range arising from the parameter range.
Working must clearly show whether permutations or combinations are used. Casework must list all cases.
Answers must lie within the principal range of the relevant inverse trig function. Out-of-range answers receive 0.
Both horizontal and vertical components must be set up separately. Units (m, s) must be stated.
Continuity correction must be applied and shown explicitly. Conditions np ≥ 5 and nq ≥ 5 must be verified.

generalPrinciples: Marks are awarded for correct mathematical reasoning and working, not just the final answer
Where a question says 'show that' or 'prove', full working must be shown — the answer is given
Correct answer without working may receive zero marks for multi-mark questions
If a student makes an error early but carries through consistently, follow-through marks may be awarded
Diagrams must be clearly labelled to receive associated marks
Rounding: round only at the final step unless otherwise instructed
Units must be included where applicable
General solutions to trig equations must include 'n ∈ ℤ' to receive full marks
Arbitrary constants in differential equation solutions must be shown

sectionI.marking: No working required. 1 mark for correct answer, 0 otherwise.

sectionII.description: Extended response questions requiring full working
sectionII.marking: Criteria-based marking. See individual question entries in ext1-questions.json for full criteria.
generalNotes: Show all necessary working
Marks may be awarded for correct intermediate steps even if the final answer is wrong
State conclusions in the context of the problem
For vector questions, clearly distinguish between scalar and vector quantities
For proofs, begin from one side only and work to the other

2023-ME1-Q11a.keySkill: binomial theorem general term and coefficient extraction

2023-ME1-Q11b.keySkill: inverse function — swap x and y, state domain

2023-ME1-Q12.keySkill: related rates using chain rule — sphere volume

2023-ME1-Q14.keySkill: dot product and angle between vectors

2023-ME1-Q15.keySkill: inverse trig integration — arctan and arcsin with substitution

2023-ME1-Q16.keySkill: Vieta's formulas — sum/product of roots of cubic

2023-ME1-Q18.keySkill: volume of revolution about x-axis — disc method

2023-ME1-Q19.keySkill: projectile motion — max height, range, position vector

2023-ME1-Q20.keySkill: separable ODE — logistic growth with partial fractions

2023-ME1-Q21.keySkill: normal approximation to binomial with continuity correction

2023-ME1-Q22.keySkill: parametric to Cartesian, reciprocal graph sketch, inequality
